verses.1: My son, pay attention to my wisdom, Incline your ear to my understanding,
verses.2: So that you may maintain discretion And your lips may comply with knowledge.
verses.3: For the lips of an adulteress drip honey, And her speech is smoother than oil;
verses.4: But in the end she is bitter as wormwood, Sharp as a two-edged sword.
verses.5: Her feet go down to death, Her steps take hold of Sheol.
verses.6: She does not ponder the path of life; Her ways are unstable, she does not know it.
verses.7: Now then, my sons, listen to me And do not turn away from the words of my mouth.
verses.8: Keep your way far from her, And do not go near the door of her house,
verses.9: Otherwise you will give your vigor to others, And your years to the cruel one;
verses.10: And strangers will be filled with your strength, And your hard-earned possessions will go to the house of a foreigner;
verses.11: And you will groan in the end, When your flesh and your body are consumed;
verses.12: And you say, “How I hated instruction! And my heart disdainfully rejected rebuke!
verses.13: “I did not listen to the voice of my teachers, Nor incline my ear to my instructors!
verses.14: “I was almost in total ruin In the midst of the assembly and congregation.”
verses.15: Drink water from your own cistern, And fresh water from your own well.
verses.16: Should your springs overflow into the street, Streams of water in the public squares?
verses.17: Let them be yours alone, And not for strangers with you.
verses.18: Let your fountain be blessed, And rejoice in the wife of your youth.
verses.19: Like a loving doe and a graceful mountain goat, Let her breasts satisfy you at all times; Be exhilarated always with her love.
verses.20: For why should you, my son, be exhilarated with an adulteress, And embrace the breasts of a foreigner?
verses.21: For the ways of everyone are before the eyes of the LORD, And He observes all his paths.
verses.22: His own wrongdoings will trap the wicked, And he will be held by the ropes of his sin.
verses.23: He will die for lack of instruction, And in the greatness of his foolishness he will go astray.
